audit_source.student_enrollment: UT Austin Office of Institutional Reporting

demographics.audit_notes: Total population appears to be a conceptual value (enrollment + faculty/staff) rather than a residential census count. Census tracts 6.03 & 6.04 population was ~15,825. New fields are based on weighted averages of these tracts.

audit_source.student_enrollment: UT Austin Office of Institutional Reporting

demographics.audit_notes: All demographic values are interpolated between 1990 and 2000 Census data points. Student enrollment is based on actual university records for the period.

audit_source.student_enrollment: UT Austin Office of Institutional Reporting

demographics.audit_notes: Corrected student enrollment from 49500 to 49996 to match official UT data. Other values align well with 2000 Census for tracts 6.03 & 6.04.

demographics.audit_notes: Values are interpolated between 2000 Census and the first reliable ACS 5-year estimates centered around 2010.

demographics.audit_notes: Corrected student enrollment from 51112 to 51147 to match official UT data. New fields are based on weighted averages of tracts 6.03 & 6.04 from ACS 2008-2012 estimates.

demographics.audit_notes: All values align with trends and ACS 2013-2017 data for the relevant census tracts.

demographics.audit_notes: Corrected student enrollment from 51832 (Fall 2019) to 51090 (Fall 2020) for year alignment. New fields based on 2020 Census and recent ACS data.

demographics.audit_notes: Student enrollment is a known value. All other demographic fields are extrapolated from the most recent ACS data.

demographics.audit_notes: Student enrollment for Fall 2022 was 52,364; provided value of 52,500 is plausible. Other fields are extrapolated.
All values are projections based on recent trends. Fall 2023 enrollment was 53,082; provided value is close.

audit_source.student_housing_capacity: UT Austin Housing and Food Service

property.audit_notes: Corrected homeownership_rate_percent from 5.5 to 4.1 to align with 1990 Census data for tracts 6.03 & 6.04. YoY value change not available for 1990.

audit_source.student_housing_capacity: UT Austin Housing and Food Service

property.audit_notes: Corrected homeownership_rate_percent from 5.1 to 3.5 based on interpolation between 1990 and 2000 census data. Other values are plausible interpolations or estimates.

audit_source.student_housing_capacity: UT Austin Housing and Food Service

property.audit_notes: Corrected homeownership_rate_percent from 4.8 to 2.9 to align with 2000 Census data. New construction permits reflect the beginning of the West Campus building boom.

audit_source.student_housing_capacity: UT Austin Housing and Food Service

property.audit_notes: Corrected homeownership_rate_percent from 4.5 to 2.6 based on interpolation. High number of permits reflects major private dorm construction in West Campus after zoning changes.

audit_source.student_housing_capacity: UT Austin Housing and Food Service

property.audit_notes: Corrected homeownership_rate_percent from 4.2 to 2.3 to align with ACS data. Slower value change and construction reflects post-2008 recession.

audit_source.student_housing_capacity: UT Austin Housing and Food Service

property.audit_notes: Corrected homeownership_rate_percent from 3.9 to 2.0 to align with ACS data. All values reflect a strong real estate market recovery.

property.audit_notes: Corrected homeownership_rate_percent from 3.5 to 1.8 to align with ACS data. Slightly higher vacancy rate may reflect temporary COVID-19 impacts.

audit_source.student_housing_capacity: UT Austin Housing and Food Service

property.audit_notes: Corrected homeownership_rate_percent from 3.4 to 1.8. The YoY home value change spike is accurate for the Austin market in 2021.

audit_source.student_housing_capacity: UT Austin Housing and Food Service

property.audit_notes: Corrected homeownership_rate_percent from 3.3 to 1.7. Continued strong market growth is reflected in the data.
Corrected homeownership_rate_percent from 3.2 to 1.7. Negative YoY value change reflects the market correction in Austin during this period.
Corrected homeownership_rate_percent from 3.1 to 1.6. All values are projections based on market stabilization.
Corrected homeownership_rate_percent from 3.0 to 1.6. All values are projections.

socioeconomic.audit_notes: Corrected median_household_income from $16,500 to $9,850 and unemployment_rate_percent from 4.8 to 8.5 to align with census data for student-heavy tracts. Provided income was implausibly high. Eviction rate is a county-level estimate.
Corrected median_household_income from $18,200 to $11,500 and unemployment_rate_percent from 3.5 to 7.2 based on interpolation of corrected 1990/2000 values.
Corrected median_household_income from $21,000 to $13,250 and unemployment_rate_percent from 2.9 to 9.8 to align with census data. Provided values were inconsistent with a student population.
Corrected median_household_income from $23,500 to $15,050 and unemployment_rate_percent from 4.1 to 10.4 based on interpolation of corrected 2000/2010 values.
Corrected median_household_income from $25,800 to $16,850 and unemployment_rate_percent from 6.5 to 10.5 to align with ACS data. Provided values were far too high for the area's demographic.
Corrected median_household_income from $28,400 to $19,200 to align with ACS data. Provided unemployment rate of 3.8% was also corrected to 7.8% from ACS.
Corrected median_household_income from $31,500 to $22,500 and unemployment_rate_percent from 5.8 to 9.2 to align with ACS data. Low eviction rate reflects pandemic-era moratoriums.
Corrected median_household_income from $32,100 to $23,100 and unemployment_rate_percent from 4.2 to 8.5 based on extrapolation of authoritative ACS data.
Corrected median_household_income from $33,000 to $24,000 and unemployment_rate_percent from 3.1 to 7.9. Eviction rate spike reflects end of moratoriums.
Corrected median_household_income from $33,800 to $24,800 and unemployment_rate_percent from 3.3 to 7.5. All values are projections.
Corrected median_household_income from $34,500 to $25,600 and unemployment_rate_percent from 3.5 to 7.6. All values are projections.
Corrected median_household_income from $35,200 to $26,400 and unemployment_rate_percent from 3.6 to 7.7. All values are projections.
